$comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the internal bridge RESPONSE envelope shape (BridgeResp). The runtime validator is the hand-written Zod schema in packages/shared/src/envelope.ts, whose z.toJSONSchema() output is diffed against this file in CI (the equivalence test) - so the TS type is inferred, not hand-synced. `error` here is the extension-side free-text string; the cross-process stable error CODES live in contracts/errors.json and are applied on the Rust side (see docs/architecture.md, section 11.1).
title: BridgeResp
description: The response posted back from the extension to the native host over the Port. Shape: { id, ok, data?, error? }.

id.description: Correlation id, echoed from the matching BridgeReq. Integers are bounded to the JS-safe range (see bridge-request.schema.json's id).

ok.description: Whether the op succeeded. When false, `error` carries a human-readable reason.

data.description: The op's payload on success. Shape varies per op; intentionally unconstrained (`unknown` on the TS side).

error.description: Human-readable failure reason when ok is false. The stable programmatic code is assigned from contracts/errors.json on the Rust side.
